{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "LPG Plant Manager",
  "description": "Rainoil Limited is a dynamic energy company recognised for safe and sustainable operations across the downstream sector of the oil and gas industry.&nbsp;Specializing in the bulk storage, retail and distribution of petroleum products, our facilities include 3 ultra-modern bulk storage terminals in Lagos, Delta and Cross River State, over 190 retail stations and a fleet of over 400 distribution trucks. Established with a commitment to delivering high-quality fuel and energy solutions in Nigeria, Rainoil has grown to become a trusted name in the industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: LPG Plant Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Usiefrun, Delta
Job Type: Full Time
Specialization: LPG (Gas)

Job Summary


	The candidate is responsible for overseeing the managing, operating and safety of the company&rsquo;s Gas plant.


Key Duties


	Manages all Gas Plant operations as well as staff-related matters.
	Maintains excellent standards in all operational activity, driving sales and profitability through the effective deployment and development of LPG cashier and operators in line with approved budgets.
	Ensures that the due process is carried out in the discharge of products and puts in various controls to minimise fraud.
	Facilitates daily stock requests from the LPG terminal.
	Implement measures to ensure seamless and safe receipt, storage, handling, and dispatch of products in the plant.
	Facilitate regular facility inspection and maintain high standard HSE and implement maintenance controls/measures.
	Monitor and report on market trends, competitor activities, consumer preference to guide Rainoil LPG offering accordingly.
	Report on LPG sales / financial results to LPG Coordinator.
	Monitors and evaluates plant operational and marketing results and develops interventions to promote business development and growth.
	Evaluation of daily sales of LPG and lodgements
	Compiles and analyses periodic sales records and consolidates such reports to the LPG Coordinator.
	Manage all technical aspects of operations of the LPG plant including the storage facility.


Requirements


	Candidates should possess a&nbsp;Second-Class Upper (B.sc) and HND Upper Credit in Engineering is required with&nbsp;1-4 years of experience.
	Honesty, commitment, hardworking and self-motivation.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their Resume to:&nbsp;recruitment@rainoil.com&nbsp;using the Position and Location as the subject of the email.",
  "datePosted": "2026-05-09",
  "validThrough": "2026-05-16",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "RainOil Limited"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Delta",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },
  "identifier": {
    "@type": "PropertyValue",
    "name": "",
    "value": "198655"
  },
  "baseSalary": {
    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",
    "currency": "NGN",
    "value": {
      "@type": "QuantitativeValue",
      "minValue": 0,
      "maxValue": 0,
      "unitText": "MONTH"
    }
  }
}



LPG Plant Manager Job at Rainoil Limited




        
    

        
        Rainoil Limited is a dynamic energy company recognised for safe and sustainable operations across the downstream sector of the oil and gas industry. Specializing in the bulk storage, retail and distribution of petroleum products, our facilities include 3 ultra-modern bulk storage terminals in Lagos, Delta and Cross River State, over 190 retail stations and a fleet of over 400 distribution trucks. Established with a commitment to delivering high-quality fuel and energy solutions in Nigeria, Rainoil has grown to become a trusted name in the industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: LPG Plant Manager
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Usiefrun, Delta
Job Type: Full Time
Specialization: LPG (Gas)

Job Summary


	The candidate is responsible for overseeing the managing, operating and safety of the company’s Gas plant.


Key Duties


	Manages all Gas Plant operations as well as staff-related matters.
	Maintains excellent standards in all operational activity, driving sales and profitability through the effective deployment and development of LPG cashier and operators in line with approved budgets.
	Ensures that the due process is carried out in the discharge of products and puts in various controls to minimise fraud.
	Facilitates daily stock requests from the LPG terminal.
	Implement measures to ensure seamless and safe receipt, storage, handling, and dispatch of products in the plant.
	Facilitate regular facility inspection and maintain high standard HSE and implement maintenance controls/measures.
	Monitor and report on market trends, competitor activities, consumer preference to guide Rainoil LPG offering accordingly.
	Report on LPG sales / financial results to LPG Coordinator.
	Monitors and evaluates plant operational and marketing results and develops interventions to promote business development and growth.
	Evaluation of daily sales of LPG and lodgements
	Compiles and analyses periodic sales records and consolidates such reports to the LPG Coordinator.
	Manage all technical aspects of operations of the LPG plant including the storage facility.


Requirements


	Candidates should possess a Second-Class Upper (B.sc) and HND Upper Credit in Engineering is required with 1-4 years of experience.
	Honesty, commitment, hardworking and self-motivation.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their Resume to: [email protected] using the Position and Location as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Front Desk Officer",
  "description": "Naji Realties is a leading hospitality and real estate company in Nigeria, dedicated to providing exceptional services and experiences that exceed our customers&#39; expectations. With a strong presence in Abuja and Lagos, our experts are passionate about delivering top-notch solutions in property development, management, and hospitality.

If you share our values and passion for excellence, we invite you to explore career opportunities with us. At Naji Realties, we foster a culture of growth, learning, and collaboration. Be part of our dynamic team and help us shape the future of hospitality and real estate in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Front Desk Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Utako, Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	We are seeking a highly skilled and enthusiastic Front Desk Officer to join our hospitality team.
	As the first point of contact for our guests, you will be responsible for delivering exceptional customer service, ensuring a warm and welcoming experience, and providing efficient check-in and check-out processes.


Key Responsibilities


	Greet and welcome guests, respond to their inquiries and resolve any issues promptly
	Manage room assignments, check-in, and check-out processes
	Handle guest registrations, payments, and departures
	Maintain accurate records of guest information, room assignments, and billing
	Provide information on hotel services, facilities, and local attractions
	Coordinate with other departments to ensure seamless service delivery
	Handle guest complaints and concerns with professionalism and courtesy
	Maintain a clean and organized front desk area
	Perform other duties as assigned by the Front Desk Manager.


Requirements


	Must have a Degree in any relevant field
	Must have at least two years of experience working in a hospitality company.
	Must reside in Abuja, preferably around Utako
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and customer service skills
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and multitask
	Proficiency in hotel software and systems (e.g., Property Management System)
	Strong problem-solving and conflict resolution skills
	Ability to maintain confidentiality and handle sensitive information
	Flexibility to work varying shifts, including nights, weekends, and holiday.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;careers@najirealties.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-06-01",

  },

Front Desk Officer Job at Naji Realties




        
    

        
        Naji Realties is a leading hospitality and real estate company in Nigeria, dedicated to providing exceptional services and experiences that exceed our customers' expectations. With a strong presence in Abuja and Lagos, our experts are passionate about delivering top-notch solutions in property development, management, and hospitality.

If you share our values and passion for excellence, we invite you to explore career opportunities with us. At Naji Realties, we foster a culture of growth, learning, and collaboration. Be part of our dynamic team and help us shape the future of hospitality and real estate in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Front Desk Officer
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Utako, Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	We are seeking a highly skilled and enthusiastic Front Desk Officer to join our hospitality team.
	As the first point of contact for our guests, you will be responsible for delivering exceptional customer service, ensuring a warm and welcoming experience, and providing efficient check-in and check-out processes.


Key Responsibilities


	Greet and welcome guests, respond to their inquiries and resolve any issues promptly
	Manage room assignments, check-in, and check-out processes
	Handle guest registrations, payments, and departures
	Maintain accurate records of guest information, room assignments, and billing
	Provide information on hotel services, facilities, and local attractions
	Coordinate with other departments to ensure seamless service delivery
	Handle guest complaints and concerns with professionalism and courtesy
	Maintain a clean and organized front desk area
	Perform other duties as assigned by the Front Desk Manager.


Requirements


	Must have a Degree in any relevant field
	Must have at least two years of experience working in a hospitality company.
	Must reside in Abuja, preferably around Utako
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and customer service skills
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and multitask
	Proficiency in hotel software and systems (e.g., Property Management System)
	Strong problem-solving and conflict resolution skills
	Ability to maintain confidentiality and handle sensitive information
	Flexibility to work varying shifts, including nights, weekends, and holiday.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Job Position: Business Development Manager

Industry: Car Rental / Mobility Services

Job Summary


	The Business Development Manager will be responsible for driving revenue growth by identifying new business opportunities, developing strategic partnerships, and expanding the company&rsquo;s corporate and individual client base within Lagos and its environs.
	The role focuses on sales strategy, client acquisition, contract management, and market expansion for car rental and chauffeured mobility services.


Key Responsibilities
Business Growth &amp; Sales:


	Identify, prospect, and secure new corporate, government, and individual clients for car rental services.
	Develop and execute business development strategies to increase fleet utilization and revenue.
	Prepare and present compelling proposals, bids, and service packages to prospective clients.
	Negotiate contracts, pricing, and service-level agreements (SLAs) with clients and partners.
	Build and maintain strong, long-term relationships with corporate clients, travel agencies, hotels, event planners, and logistics firms.
	Serve as the primary point of contact for key accounts, ensuring high levels of customer satisfaction and repeat business.
	Gather client feedback and work closely with operations to improve service delivery.
	Conduct market research to identify trends, competitor activities, and new opportunities within Lagos and Nigeria&rsquo;s mobility sector.
	Recommend new routes to market, service offerings (e.g., long-term leasing, executive chauffeured services), and pricing strategies.
	Track and report on sales pipelines, conversion rates, and revenue performance.
	Develop strategic partnerships with hotels, airlines, corporate organizations, and tourism-related businesses in Ikeja and across Lagos.
	Represent the company at networking events, trade shows, and industry meetings.
	Work closely with operations, fleet management, and finance teams to ensure seamless service delivery.
	Prepare regular sales reports, forecasts, and performance updates for senior management.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and relevant regulatory requirements.


Required Qualifications, Skills &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Transport Management, or a related field.
	Minimum of 3&ndash;5 years of experience in business development, sales, or commercial roles (experience in car rental, logistics, travel, or transportation is a strong advantage).

	Excellent negotiation, communication, and presentation skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
	Strategic thinking and problem-solving
	Relationship management and networking
	Strong commercial and financial awareness
	Ability to work independently and under pressure
	Customer-focused mindset
	Data-driven decision-making


Preferred Attributes:


	Experience dealing with corporate fleets or mobility services
	Existing network of corporate or institutional clients in Lagos
	Valid Nigerian driver&rsquo;s license (an added advantage).


Compensation &amp; Benefits


	Competitive salary
	Performance-based commissions/bonuses
	Opportunities for career growth within the organization

Business Development Manager Job at Nextint Global Consult




        
    

        
        Nextint Global Consult into Managment Consulting is recruiting suitably qualified candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Manager

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Car Rental / Mobility Services

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Business Development Manager will be responsible for driving revenue growth by identifying new business opportunities, developing strategic partnerships, and expanding the company’s corporate and individual client base within Lagos and its environs.
	The role focuses on sales strategy, client acquisition, contract management, and market expansion for car rental and chauffeured mobility services.


Key Responsibilities
Business Growth & Sales:


	Identify, prospect, and secure new corporate, government, and individual clients for car rental services.
	Develop and execute business development strategies to increase fleet utilization and revenue.
	Prepare and present compelling proposals, bids, and service packages to prospective clients.
	Negotiate contracts, pricing, and service-level agreements (SLAs) with clients and partners.
	Build and maintain strong, long-term relationships with corporate clients, travel agencies, hotels, event planners, and logistics firms.
	Serve as the primary point of contact for key accounts, ensuring high levels of customer satisfaction and repeat business.
	Gather client feedback and work closely with operations to improve service delivery.
	Conduct market research to identify trends, competitor activities, and new opportunities within Lagos and Nigeria’s mobility sector.
	Recommend new routes to market, service offerings (e.g., long-term leasing, executive chauffeured services), and pricing strategies.
	Track and report on sales pipelines, conversion rates, and revenue performance.
	Develop strategic partnerships with hotels, airlines, corporate organizations, and tourism-related businesses in Ikeja and across Lagos.
	Represent the company at networking events, trade shows, and industry meetings.
	Work closely with operations, fleet management, and finance teams to ensure seamless service delivery.
	Prepare regular sales reports, forecasts, and performance updates for senior management.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and relevant regulatory requirements.


Required Qualifications, Skills & Experience


	Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Transport Management, or a related field.
	Minimum of 3–5 years of experience in business development, sales, or commercial roles (experience in car rental, logistics, travel, or transportation is a strong advantage).
	Proven track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets.
	Strong understanding of the Lagos business environment and corporate market.
	Excellent negotiation, communication, and presentation skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
	Strategic thinking and problem-solving
	Relationship management and networking
	Strong commercial and financial awareness
	Ability to work independently and under pressure
	Customer-focused mindset
	Data-driven decision-making


Preferred Attributes:


	Experience dealing with corporate fleets or mobility services
	Existing network of corporate or institutional clients in Lagos
	Valid Nigerian driver’s license (an added advantage).


Compensation & Benefits


	Competitive salary
	Performance-based commissions/bonuses
	Opportunities for career growth within the organization
